title: Past the Surface, Sorrow
scripture_text: The Lord is near to the brokenhearted.
reflection: Death is not the last word. The shadow has a length but not a depth. Walk slowly. There is no rush to be on the other side of grief. (The fourth hearing of this day.)
body:
Find a place where no one can see you. Let the tears come if they want to come. They have been waiting.

You do not have to pray the right words. You do not have to be strong. You do not have to make sense of this. The Lord is your shepherd; even here, in the valley, He is here.

Read the psalm slowly. The words are old. They have been said over a billion grieving mouths before yours. They are still true. They will be true tomorrow.

Your good shepherd is not ahead of you, beckoning. He is beside you, walking. Or behind you, guarding. Sometimes you cannot see Him. That does not mean He is not there.

When you are ready, set one foot in front of the other. There is no hurry. The valley is not infinite, even when it feels infinite.

Grief does not have a deadline. Grief is not a project to be finished. Grief is the long, slow re-orienting of the heart to a world that has changed shape. The shape will not be what it was. The shape will be something new, with an empty chair in it, or a name you cannot say, or a song you cannot listen to. The shape will be yours, and the shape will be enough.

The Lord does not rush grief. The Lord is the one who stood at the tomb of His friend and wept, even though He knew what was about to happen. He is the one who said, "Blessed are those who mourn." He is the one who is close to the brokenhearted.

You are not behind. You are not slow. You are walking the long road at the pace the long road requires. The Lord is walking with you.

There is no hurry. The road is long, and the Lord is with you on it. Walk at the pace the road requires.

This is the fourth time. You have gone deeper now than you went the first time. The well is deeper. The water is still. The Lord is at the bottom. Keep going.
prayer: Lord, walk with me through this. I cannot see the path. Be my feet when mine will not move. Amen.
